gradeLabel: Open weights with a restricted/custom license

ladderVerdict.justification: The Gemma Terms permit commercial use and redistribution of the weights provided the Gemma Terms are attached, but prohibit using the weights to train or improve other models, exceeding 1 million monthly users without a separate Google license, and removing or modifying the Gemma Terms. This restricted/custom license places Gemma 3 at L1 on the OMSF Openness Ladder.

license.name: Gemma Terms of Use
allowed: Commercial use within the Gemma Terms
Redistribution of the weights with the Gemma Terms attached
notAllowed: Use the weights to train or improve other models
Exceed 1 million monthly users without a Google license
Remove or modify the Gemma Terms
license.scaleTrigger: Google license required above 1 million monthly users
license.commercialNote: Commercial use is permitted under the Gemma Terms. If a product or service has more than 1 million monthly users, you must request a license from Google.

deployment.verdict: runs on a single consumer GPU

technical.quantizationImpact: For the 27B variant, full BF16 precision requires ~54 GB VRAM; quantizing to GGUF Q4_K_M reduces this to ~14‑16 GB (≈4‑bit). Similar proportional savings apply to the 12B and 4B variants.

private.text: Private individuals can use Gemma 3 under the Gemma Terms for personal projects, research, or prototyping. Commercial use is allowed as long as the resulting service does not exceed 1 million monthly active users, and the weights must not be used to train other models.

enterprise.text: Enterprises may deploy Gemma 3 commercially, but must monitor monthly active users; exceeding 1 million MAU triggers a requirement to obtain a separate license from Google. Additionally, the license forbids using the weights to train or improve other models. These obligations necessitate compliance tracking.

nonprofit.text: Nonprofit organizations enjoy the same commercial‑use permission as enterprises, subject to the 1 million MAU threshold and the prohibition on using the weights to train other models. If the nonprofit’s public‑facing application stays below the threshold, usage is unrestricted.

sources.title: Google DeepMind Releases Gemma 3 with Open Weights and Multimodal
Gemma 3 QAT Models: Bringing state-of-the-Art AI to Consumer GPUs
